tickerText: [TENNIS] HSBC Championships (Queen's): Donna Vekic defeats Emma Raducanu (6-0, 7-6) as a lucky loser for her first WTA 500 title; Alex de Minaur faces Denis Shapovalov in men's round of 16 today — Adrian Mannarino upsets Jakub Mensik • [BADMINTON] Sands China Macau Open underway (June 16-21): men's and women's singles round of 32 in progress at the Macau East Asian Games Dome • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals open in Paris today at Centquatre-Paris — Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi lead stacked draws in the season-ending $710K event's European debut • [PICKLEBALL] Capital Securities Beijing Open launches at China's National Tennis Center (June 17-21); Zane Ford makes PPA Tour Asia debut as men's singles top seed • [TABLE TENNIS] Kanak Jha climbs to world No. 24 ahead of United States Smash (June 26, Los Angeles); ITTF announces Shanghai will host the 2026 World Table Tennis for Health Festival in October

newsItems.title: Vekic Sweeps Raducanu for Queen's WTA 500 Title
newsItems.excerpt: Lucky loser Donna Vekic defeated Emma Raducanu 6-0, 7-6 (8-6) to win her first WTA 500 crown at Queen's Club — her biggest title since 2023. On the men's side, top seed Alex de Minaur meets Denis Shapovalov in the round of 16 today after Adrian Mannarino stunned third seed Jakub Mensik.

newsItems.title: Macau Open Underway as BWF World Tour Shifts to Asia
newsItems.excerpt: The Sands China Ltd. Macau Open began June 16 at the Macau East Asian Games Dome, with round-of-32 action across all five disciplines on June 17. The Super 300 event carries a $370,000 prize pool and runs through June 21 before the tour heads to the U.S. Open in late June.

newsItems.title: PSA Squash Tour Finals Kick Off in Paris
newsItems.excerpt: The season-ending PSA Squash Tour Finals opened June 17 at Centquatre-Paris — the event's first staging in France and first in Europe since 2013. World champions Mostafa Asal and Amina Orfi head stacked men's and women's draws on an all-glass ASB ShowCourt, with $710,000 in prize money at stake through June 20.

newsItems.title: Beijing Open Brings PPA Tour Asia to Olympic Venue
newsItems.excerpt: The Capital Securities Beijing Open launched June 17 at China's National Tennis Center — the same Olympic grounds that hosted tennis superstars — marking pickleball's biggest stage yet in the capital. U.S. top-10 player Zane Ford debuts as men's singles No. 1 seed, with Chao Yi Wang and Sahra Dennehy leading the women's draw through June 21.

newsItems.title: Jha Rises to No. 24 as US Smash and Shanghai Festival Loom
newsItems.excerpt: Kanak Jha jumped six spots to world No. 24 after his Skopje runner-up finish, positioning the American among the homegrown stars set for United States Smash at the Ontario Convention Center (June 26–July 5). The ITTF Foundation also announced the 2026 World Table Tennis for Health Festival will head to Shanghai in October.
